2605.15118.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
2605.15118.paper_summary: We introduce a reusable framework for auditing whether LLM attack benchmarks collectively cover the threat surface: a 4$\times$6 Target $\times$ Technique matrix grounded in STRIDE, constructed from a 507-leaf taxonomy -- 401 data-populated and 106 threat-model-derived leaves -- of inference-time attacks extracted from 932 arXiv security studies (2023--2026). The matrix enables benchmark-external validation -- auditing collective coverage rather than individual benchmark consistency. Applying it to six public benchmarks reveals that the three primary frameworks (HarmBench, InjecAgent, AgentDojo) occupy non-overlapping cells covering at most 25\% of the matrix, while entire STRIDE threat categories (Service Disruption, Model Internals) lack any standardized evaluation, despite published attacks in these categories achieving 46$\times$ token amplification and 96\% attack success rates through mechanisms which no benchmark tests. The corpus of 2,521 unique attack groups further reveals pervasive naming fragmentation (up to 29 surface forms for a single attack) and heavy concentration in Safety \& Alignment Bypass, structural properties invisible at smaller scale. The taxonomy, attack records, and coverage mappings are released as extensible artifacts; as new benchmarks emerge, they can be mapped onto the same matrix, enabling the community to track whether evaluation gaps are closing.
2605.15118.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了一個可重用的框架，用於審核 LLM 攻擊基準是否共同覆蓋威脅面：一個基於 STRIDE 的 4$\times$6 目標 $\times$ 技術矩陣，該矩陣由 507 個葉子組成的分類法構建而成——401 個數據填充的葉子和 106 個基於威脅模型的葉子——這些葉子來自 932 篇 arXiv 安全研究（2023--2026）中提取的推理時間攻擊。該矩陣使基準外部驗證成為可能——審核集體覆蓋範圍，而非個別基準的一致性。將其應用於六個公共基準顯示，三個主要框架（HarmBench、InjecAgent、AgentDojo）佔據了不重疊的單元，最多覆蓋 25\% 的矩陣，而整個 STRIDE 威脅類別（服務中斷、模型內部）缺乏任何標準化評估，儘管這些類別中已發表的攻擊通過未經測試的機制實現了 46$\times$ 的標記增幅和 96\% 的攻擊成功率。2521 個獨特攻擊組的語料庫進一步揭示了普遍的命名碎片化（單一攻擊可有多達 29 種表面形式）和在安全與對齊繞過中的高度集中，這些結構特性在較小規模下無法顯現。該分類法、攻擊記錄和覆蓋映射作為可擴展的文物發布；隨著新基準的出現，它們可以映射到相同的矩陣上，使社群能夠追蹤評估差距是否在縮小。
2605.15118.author: Karthik Raghu Iyer et.al.
2605.15118.authors: Karthik Raghu Iyer, Yazdan Jamshidi, Nicholas Bray, Alexey A. Shvets
